problem: The ticket asks for better platform abstractions across Helix Global, Netsuite, and SMB. The helix-cli is completely platform-agnostic — it has zero platform-specific gating logic. All platform differentiation is handled server-side. The CLI operates uniformly regardless of which platform the connected organization uses.

files.why: Main CLI entry point with command routing. No platform branching — all commands operate uniformly.
Config types (HxConfig, OrgEntry, MultiTokenConfig). No platform field in config — only authentication and org identity.
HTTP client (hxFetch). Token format detection (hxi_ prefix) but no platform branching.

facts: Zero instances of platform-specific gating (NETSUITE, GENERAL, SMB, helix-global, helix-netsuite, helix-smb) found in helix-cli source code.
CLI config types (HxConfig, OrgEntry, MultiTokenConfig) contain no platform field.
All commands (login, org, tickets, inspect, comments, token, update) operate uniformly regardless of connected org platform.
Only conditional logic: token format detection (hxi_ prefix), OS detection for browser opening (process.platform), and org matching by ID/alias/name.
Build: tsc. Typecheck: tsc --noEmit. No lint or test runner configured.
unknowns: Whether a future platform abstraction would need to surface in the CLI (e.g., platform-aware commands or output formatting).
Whether the CLI should be aware of platform capabilities for command validation before sending to server.
